what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML presentation page titled as a component bench for cards, showing six card-like UI specimens in a 3-by-2 grid with local CSS styling and runtime-injected icons.
observations: Imports Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts plus shared /surface stylesheets for piece and phone styling.
Creates an absolute-positioned .bench grid with six .cell panels, each containing a labeled component specimen.
Specimens shown are The Piece Card, The Row, The Couplet, The Chip, The Person Row, and The Frosted Card.
Styling relies heavily on CSS custom properties such as --dna-space-26, --warm, --cool, --nested, --bronze, --gold, and --line.
Several specimen details use inline style attributes for swatches, dots, font sizing, and the frosted-card background.
Loads /surface/runtime/organisms.js and fills icon placeholders i1 through i6 with DNA.org.icon calls.
standouts: The page treats cards as a component taxonomy rather than as one finished application screen.
The six examples cover multiple card densities: compact rows, statistic display, identity rows, chips, palette cards, and frosted content.
The layout presents components as hand-scale specimens centered within larger labeled cells.
conventions: Uses a single-file HTML structure with embedded page-specific CSS in the head.
Follows a CSS-variable-driven design vocabulary with dna-prefixed spacing, radius, and type tokens.
Uses shared decorative/page elements such as .view, .bp, .title, .mark, and .foot.
Uses runtime icon injection instead of static icon markup in the HTML.
Breaks strict CSS separation by placing several visual values in inline style attributes.
concerns: The bench uses fixed absolute offsets and fixed specimen widths, so responsive behavior is dependent on external CSS not visible in this file.
The inline script assumes DNA.org.icon is available and that all i1 through i6 elements exist.
Inline styles make some specimen presentation details harder to centralize or reuse from this file alone.
notes: This file is a visual specimen board for card component patterns, using six labeled examples inside a designed bench layout.
questions: What behavior or fallback is intended if /surface/runtime/organisms.js fails to load?
Are the inline specimen styles intentional examples, or should they map to reusable classes?
How much of the responsive layout is expected to come from /surface/styles/phone.css?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it presents a component bench focused on card variants.
intention_signals: It seems intended to document and display a small vocabulary of reusable card and row components as visual parts within a broader design system.
novelty: The unusual aspect is the bench-like presentation of UI components as labeled material specimens, with each card type staged inside a larger cell.
contribution: This file contributes a compact catalog of card, row, chip, statistic, identity, and frosted-card treatments, plus the presentation pattern of arranging component specimens in a labeled grid.
summary_for_embedding: A standalone HTML component bench page that displays six card-related UI specimens in a styled 3-by-2 grid using dna design tokens and runtime-injected icons.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to show the range and handling of card-like components as reusable design substrates within a visual ledger.
